---
title: "multivariate"
format: html
---

```{r}
# Install pacman if missing, then use it to load/install required packages
if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(car, lmtest, ggplot2, emmeans, gtsummary)


# load the data of the example
epi_data <-  readRDS("data_multivariate.Rds")
```

```{r}
epi_data
```


```{r}
# Fit Multiple Linear Regression
mod_multiple <- lm(sbp ~ pm25 + age +
                         sex + bmi, 
                   data = epi_data)

# Print a basic summary to the console
summary(mod_multiple)

```

```{r}
# Create a publication-ready table using gtsummary
tbl_regression(mod_multiple) |> 
  add_global_p() |> 
  bold_labels() |> 
  bold_p(t = 0.05) |> 
  modify_caption("**Table 1. Multivariable Linear Regression for Systolic Blood Pressure**")

```

```{r}
# 1. Visual Diagnostic Plots
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(mod_multiple)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1)) # Reset layout
```

```{r}
# 2. Formal Test for Homoscedasticity (Breusch-Pagan Test)
bptest(mod_multiple)
```


```{r}
# 3. Checking for Multicollinearity (Variance Inflation Factor)
vif(mod_multiple)
```

```{r}
# Fit Logistic Regression Model
mod_logistic <- glm(malaria_pos ~ rainfall + bednet + age + ses, 
                    data = epi_data, 
                    family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# Publication-ready table with Exponentiated Coefficients (Odds Ratios)
tbl_regression(mod_logistic, exponentiate = TRUE) |> 
  add_global_p() |> 
  bold_labels() |> 
  modify_caption("**Table 2. Multivariable Logistic Regression for Malaria Positivity**")
```

```{r}
# Create a hypothetical dataset to predict across a range of rainfall
new_data <- expand.grid(
  rainfall = seq(min(epi_data$rainfall), max(epi_data$rainfall), by = 5),
  bednet = c(0, 1),
  age = mean(epi_data$age), # Hold age at its mean
  ses = "Medium"            # Hold SES at its baseline
)


new_data
```


```{r}
# Predict probabilities and standard errors for confidence intervals
predictions <- predict(mod_logistic, newdata = new_data, type = "link", se.fit = TRUE)

predictions
```


```{r}



# Calculate 95% Confidence Intervals on the logit scale, back-transform to probabilities
inv_logit <- function(x) exp(x) / (1 + exp(x))
new_data$pred_prob <- inv_logit(predictions$fit)
new_data$ci_lower <- inv_logit(predictions$fit - (1.96 * predictions$se.fit))
new_data$ci_upper <- inv_logit(predictions$fit + (1.96 * predictions$se.fit))

# Format the factor for the legend
new_data$Bednet_Status <- factor(new_data$bednet, labels = c("No Bed-net", "Uses Bed-net"))

new_data
```

```{r}
ggplot(new_data, aes(x = rainfall, y = pred_prob, color = Bednet_Status, fill = Bednet_Status)) 
```

```{r}
ggplot(new_data, aes(x = rainfall, y = pred_prob, color = Bednet_Status, fill = Bednet_Status)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), alpha = 0.6, color = NA) 
```
```{r}
ggplot(new_data, aes(x = rainfall, y = pred_prob, color = Bednet_Status, fill = Bednet_Status)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2)
```
```{r}
# Build a publication-ready plot
ggplot(new_data, aes(x = rainfall, y = pred_prob, color = Bednet_Status, fill = Bednet_Status)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#2166AC")) + # Custom elegant colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#2166AC"))
```
```{r}
ggplot(new_data, aes(x = rainfall, y = pred_prob, color = Bednet_Status, fill = Bednet_Status)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#2166AC")) + # Custom elegant colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#2166AC")) +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Probability of Malaria by Rainfall",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Age (mean) and SES (Medium)",
    x = "Cumulative Rainfall (mm)",
    y = "Predicted Probability",
    color = "Intervention",
    fill = "Intervention"
  )
```
```{r}
# Build a publication-ready plot
ggplot(new_data, aes(x = rainfall, y = pred_prob, color = Bednet_Status, fill = Bednet_Status)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#2166AC")) + # Custom elegant colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#2166AC")) +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Probability of Malaria by Rainfall",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Age (mean) and SES (Medium)",
    x = "Cumulative Rainfall (mm)",
    y = "Predicted Probability",
    color = "Intervention",
    fill = "Intervention"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) # Clean, minimal theme suitable for journals
```
```{r}
# Build a publication-ready plot
ggplot(new_data, aes(x = rainfall, y = pred_prob, color = Bednet_Status, fill = Bednet_Status)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#2166AC")) + # Custom elegant colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#2166AC")) +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Probability of Malaria by Rainfall",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Age (mean) and SES (Medium)",
    x = "Cumulative Rainfall (mm)",
    y = "Predicted Probability",
    color = "Intervention",
    fill = "Intervention"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) + # Clean, minimal theme suitable for journals
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
```


```{r}
# Build a publication-ready plot
ggplot(new_data, aes(x = rainfall, y = pred_prob, color = Bednet_Status, fill = Bednet_Status)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#2166AC")) + # Custom elegant colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#B2182B", "#2166AC")) +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Probability of Malaria by Rainfall",
    subtitle = "Adjusted for Age (mean) and SES (Medium)",
    x = "Cumulative Rainfall (mm)",
    y = "Predicted Probability",
    color = "Intervention",
    fill = "Intervention"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) + # Clean, minimal theme suitable for journals
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))


```
```{r}
# Fit a Base model vs Full model
mod_base <- glm(malaria_pos ~ rainfall + age, 
                data=epi_data, 
                family=binomial(link = "logit"))


# Publication-ready Side-by-Side Table Comparison
tbl_base <- tbl_regression(mod_base, exponentiate = TRUE) |> 
            add_global_p()

tbl_base
```

```{r}
mod_full <- glm(malaria_pos ~ rainfall + age + bednet + ses, 
                data=epi_data, 
                family=binomial(link = "logit"))


tbl_full <- tbl_regression(mod_full, exponentiate = TRUE) |> 
            add_global_p()

tbl_full
```


```{r}


tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(tbl_base, tbl_full),
  tab_spanner = c("**Base Model**", "**Fully Adjusted Model**")
)

```

```{r}
# Likelihood Ratio Test
anova(mod_base, mod_full, test = "Chisq") 
```

```{r}
# Crude effect of rainfall on Malaria
crude_model <- glm(malaria_pos ~ rainfall, data = epi_data, 
                   family = binomial(link = "logit"))

mod_logistic <- glm(malaria_pos ~ rainfall + bednet + age + ses, data = epi_data, 
                    family = binomial(link = "logit"))
```

```{r}
tbl_regression(mod_logistic, exponentiate = FALSE) |> 
            add_global_p()
```


```{r}
coef(mod_logistic) |> round(2)
```

```{r}
coef(mod_logistic)["rainfall"]
```


```{r}


crude_coef <- coef(crude_model)["rainfall"]

# Adjusted effect (controlling for age, SES, bednet)
adj_coef <- coef(mod_logistic)["rainfall"]

# Calculate % change
percent_change <- abs((crude_coef - adj_coef) / adj_coef) * 100
cat("Percentage change in rainfall coefficient:", round(percent_change, 2), "%\n")

```

```{r}
# Add interaction term (rainfall * bednet)
mod_interaction <- glm(malaria_pos ~ rainfall * bednet + age + ses, 
                       data = epi_data, family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# Print interaction table
tbl_regression(mod_interaction, exponentiate = TRUE) |>
  add_global_p() |> 
  bold_p()
```


```{r}


# Calculate marginal means at specific rainfall checkpoints
marginal_probs <- emmeans(mod_interaction, ~ bednet | rainfall, 
                          at = list(rainfall = c(70, 130)), 
                          type = "response")
marginal_probs
```

# Solution to examples

```{r}
# Code Solution
mod_bmi <- lm(bmi ~ age + sex + ses, data = epi_data)
tbl_regression(mod_bmi)
```

```{r}
# Code Solution
epi_data$pm10 <- epi_data$pm25 * 1.5 + rnorm(500, 0, 2)
mod_coll <- lm(sbp ~ pm25 + pm10 + age + sex, data = epi_data)
vif(mod_coll)
```

```{r}
# Code Solution
mod_ses_malaria <- glm(malaria_pos ~ age + ses, data = epi_data, family = binomial)
tbl_regression(mod_ses_malaria, exponentiate = TRUE)
```
